pages.name: Home
pages.description: Landing page for the game’s official news, updates, and key sections.
primary_functions: Show highlights of latest patch notes, esports news, developer blogs, and guides
Provide quick navigation to Patch Notes, Esports, Developer Blog, and Guides sections
Offer a prominent search bar or icon to start content searches (e.g., “balance patch”, “Shadow Siege”)
Expose entry points to newsletter signup and key user features like Reading List and Collections

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Patch Notes" link in the main navigation or a 'View all patch notes' CTA in the patch highlights section
Click the "Esports" or "Tournaments" highlights section
Click the "Developer Blog" link in the header or a dev diary highlight
Click the "Guides" link in the header or a guide highlight card
Click any highlighted article card (patch notes, esports, dev blog, or guide) on the homepage
Submit the global search form in the header (e.g., "balance patch" or "Shadow Siege")
Click the "Newsletter" / "Email Updates" link in the header or footer
Click the "Reading List" link in the header
Click the "Favorites" link in the header
Click the "Collections" link in the header
Click the "Notifications" link in the header or notification status chip

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Home" header link and site logo from any page
Users can enter the main site URL directly or via browser bookmarks

pages.name: Patch Notes Listing
pages.description: Listing page for all game patch notes across platforms.
primary_functions: List patch notes articles with summary, platform tags, dates, and version numbers
Filter patch notes by platform (e.g., PC, console) using a platform filter dropdown
Filter by date range, including presets like “Last 14 days”
Sort patch notes (e.g., Newest First, Oldest First, by version number)
Open an individual patch notes article in the Article Detail page

incoming_params.description: Optional platform pre-filter (e.g., 'pc', 'xbox') applied on initial load.
Optional date preset (e.g., 'last_14_days') for quickly scoping recent patch notes.
Optional explicit start date (YYYY-MM-DD) when using a custom date range.
Optional explicit end date (YYYY-MM-DD) when using a custom date range.
Optional sort key (e.g., 'newest', 'oldest', 'version_desc').
Current pagination index for the patch notes list.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a patch notes article title or card in the listing
Use the global header search while on the Patch Notes page

access_methods.description: Primary access via the "Patch Notes" link in the main navigation
Linked from homepage patch highlights or contextual "View all patch notes" CTAs
Filters and sort state can be deep-linked via query parameters

pages.name: Esports News Listing
pages.description: Listing page for esports-related news, including tournament announcements.
primary_functions: List esports news articles with key metadata (title, date, views, tags such as Tournament Announcement)
Filter esports content by date range, including custom start and end dates (e.g., 2025-01-01 to 2025-12-31)
Filter by category or tag, including a Tournament or Tournament Announcement option
Sort esports content (e.g., Most Viewed, Newest First, Oldest First)
Open an esports article (such as tournament announcements) in the Article Detail page

incoming_params.description: Optional esports category preset (e.g., 'tournaments') used by the "Tournaments" header link.
Optional date preset for esports content (e.g., 'this_year').
Custom filter start date (YYYY-MM-DD) for esports items.
Custom filter end date (YYYY-MM-DD) for esports items.
Sort key for esports items (e.g., 'most_viewed', 'newest').
Current pagination index for esports news.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click any esports article title, including tournament announcements
Submit a query via the global search while on the Esports page
Open Favorites via the header to verify tournament articles have been favorited

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Esports" and "Tournaments" header links
Linked from homepage esports highlights and in-article CTAs
Deep-linked with category and date filters via query parameters

pages.name: Developer Blog Listing
pages.description: Listing page for developer blogs, including roadmap dev diaries.
primary_functions: List developer blog posts with titles, dates, and tags (e.g., Roadmap)
Filter by tag or topic, including a Roadmap filter option
Filter by date using presets such as “Last 6 months”
Sort developer blog posts (e.g., Oldest First, Newest First within the filtered period)
Open a dev diary or roadmap post in the Article Detail page

incoming_params.description: Optional tag filter (e.g., 'roadmap') used for roadmap dev diary listing.
Optional date preset such as 'last_6_months' for roadmap reading flows.
Sort key (e.g., 'oldest', 'newest') for blog posts.
Current pagination index for developer blog posts.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click any dev diary or roadmap article title in the listing
After creating a 'Roadmap Reading' collection, open Collections from the header
Use the global search while on the Developer Blog page

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Developer Blog" header link
Linked from homepage dev blog highlights or other contextual CTAs
Deep-linked with tag and datePreset parameters for roadmap-specific lists

pages.name: Guides Listing
pages.description: Listing page for gameplay guides and how-to articles.
primary_functions: List guides with titles, audience level, estimated reading time, and ratings
Filter guides by audience (e.g., Beginner/New Players, Intermediate, Advanced)
Filter guides by reading time range (e.g., set minimum 10 minutes and maximum 30 minutes)
Sort guides by Highest Rated, Most Helpful, or Newest
Open an individual guide article in the Article Detail page

incoming_params.description: Optional audience filter (e.g., 'beginner', 'new_players') used for new player guide flows.
Minimum reading time filter in minutes (e.g., 10).
Maximum reading time filter in minutes (e.g., 30).
Sort key (e.g., 'highest_rated', 'most_helpful').
Current pagination index for guides.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a guide title or card in the Guides listing
After adding guides to a 'New Players' collection, open Collections from the header
Use the global search while on the Guides page

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Guides" / "How to Play" header link
Linked from homepage guide highlights and onboarding CTAs for new players
Deep-linked with audience and reading time filters for targeted guide lists

pages.name: Search Results
pages.description: Search results page for queries initiated from the global search bar.
primary_functions: Display search results for a given text query (e.g., “balance patch”, “Shadow Siege”)
Allow sorting of results (e.g., Newest First, Oldest First, Relevance)
Provide filters such as content category (Patch Notes, Update Announcement, Esports, Guides) and region (e.g., Europe, North America)
Allow refining by date or other relevant facets when needed
Open any search result item in the Article Detail page
Provide breadcrumbs or navigation to return to the same filtered search results list

incoming_params.description: Text query entered into the global search (e.g., 'balance patch', 'Shadow Siege').
Optional content-type filter (e.g., 'patch_notes', 'update_announcement', 'esports').
Optional region filter (e.g., 'europe', 'north_america').
Sort key (e.g., 'newest', 'oldest', 'relevance').
Optional filter start date (YYYY-MM-DD) for search results.
Optional filter end date (YYYY-MM-DD) for search results.
Current pagination index within search results.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click any search result title, including balance patches or "Shadow Siege" announcements

access_methods.description: Reached after submitting the global search form from the header on any page
Deep-linkable via the query and filter parameters

pages.name: Article Detail
pages.description: Generic detail page used to display full content for patch notes, esports news, update announcements, dev diaries, and guides.
primary_functions: Display the full article content including title, date, version (if applicable), platform, region, tags, and reading time
Provide an “Add to Reading List” or bookmark control to save/unsave the article to the user’s Reading List
Provide an “Add to Favorites” or heart control to mark/unmark the article as a favorite
Allow adding the article to a collection, including creating a new collection from within the “Add to Collection” flow
Display comments and allow the user to write and post new comments (e.g., posting the balance patch comment)
Provide breadcrumb or contextual navigation back to the originating list (Patch Notes, Esports, Developer Blog, Guides, or Search Results)

incoming_params.description: Required article ID to display full details.
Origin context (e.g., 'patch_notes', 'esports', 'dev_blog', 'guides', 'search', 'reading_list', 'favorites', 'collection', 'home') used to label breadcrumbs.
Encoded URL of the originating list page so the 'Back to results' breadcrumb can return with the same filters and sort state.

outgoing_connections.trigger: After bookmarking an article, open the Reading List from the header to verify it was added
After favoriting an esports tournament article, open Favorites from the header
After creating or updating a collection from the article page, open Collections from the header
Return to balance patch search results via the breadcrumb when 'from=search' and a returnUrl is present

access_methods.description: Accessed via article links that include an 'id' query parameter

pages.name: Reading List
pages.description: Page showing all articles the user has bookmarked or saved to read later.
primary_functions: List all articles saved via the “Add to Reading List” or bookmark action, including patch notes and announcements
Allow opening a saved article in the Article Detail page by clicking its title
Allow removing items from the Reading List
Provide basic filters or sorting (e.g., by date added, newest first, content type) to manage saved items

incoming_params.description: Optional sort key for saved items (e.g., 'date_added_desc').
Optional content-type filter to narrow the Reading List (e.g., only patch notes).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click any saved article title to reopen it from the Reading List

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Reading List" link in the main header
Linked from confirmation toasts or CTAs after bookmarking articles
Deep-linked with sort and content-type filters via query parameters

pages.name: Favorites
pages.description: Page showing all articles the user has marked as favorites.
primary_functions: List articles marked as favorites via the heart icon, including esports tournament announcements and other content
Allow opening a favored article in the Article Detail page
Allow removing articles from Favorites
Optionally filter or sort favorites (e.g., by content type or date favorited)

incoming_params.description: Optional sort key for favorites (e.g., 'date_added_desc').
Optional filter for favorites by content type (e.g., only esports tournaments).

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a favorite article title to reopen it from the Favorites page

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Favorites" link in the main header
Linked from confirmation toasts after favoriting articles
Deep-linked with sort and content-type filters via query parameters

pages.name: Collections Overview
pages.description: Overview page for user-defined collections (custom reading lists) such as Roadmap Reading and New Players.
primary_functions: Display a list of all collections created by the user (e.g., Roadmap Reading, New Players)
Allow creating a new empty collection, independent of the article page flow
Allow renaming or deleting existing collections
Open a specific collection in the Collection Detail page for deeper management

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a collection name (e.g., 'Roadmap Reading' or 'New Players') in the overview list
Immediately after creating a new collection, follow a 'View collection' CTA

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Collections" link in the main header
Linked from success messages after creating collections via article or guide flows

pages.name: Collection Detail
pages.description: Detail view of a single collection, listing all included articles.
primary_functions: List all articles included in a specific collection, such as Roadmap Reading or New Players
Allow opening any article in the Article Detail page
Allow removing individual articles from the collection
Allow editing the collection’s name and viewing its metadata (e.g., number of items)

incoming_params.description: Required collection ID whose contents and metadata will be displayed.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click any article title inside the collection
Use a 'Back to all collections' link or the header navigation

access_methods.description: Accessed by links that include a 'collectionId' query parameter
Opened from Collections Overview or from in-context success messages

pages.name: Newsletter Preferences
pages.description: Settings page for subscribing to or managing email newsletter updates.
primary_functions: Allow the user to enter or update an email address for newsletters
Provide a frequency dropdown with options such as Daily, Weekly, and Monthly
Allow selecting specific topics or categories (e.g., Patch Notes, Esports, Promotions, Community Stories) and deselecting unwanted ones
Allow specifying a region (e.g., North America, Europe) if available
Submit and save newsletter preferences via a Subscribe or Save Preferences button and show a confirmation message

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the site logo or a 'Back to Home' link after successfully saving newsletter preferences

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Newsletter" link in the main header
Accessible via the "Newsletter" link in the footer
Linked from homepage CTAs or confirmation banners suggesting email updates
Preferences are applied on form submission via the Subscribe/Save button

pages.name: Notification Settings
pages.description: Page for configuring in-site and other notification preferences.
primary_functions: Display a list of notification categories such as Server Status, Maintenance, Promotional Offers, Esports News, and others
Allow toggling individual categories on or off, including turning on only Server Status and Maintenance and turning off all others
Allow selecting delivery methods (e.g., In-site Notifications, Email, Mobile Push) and enabling or disabling each
Provide controls to set Do Not Disturb or quiet hours (e.g., start 22:00, end 08:00)
Save notification preferences with a Save Changes button and display a success confirmation

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the site logo after saving notification settings to return to the homepage

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Notifications" link in the main header and profile/notification icon
Linked from support articles about managing notifications
Settings are updated on Save Changes form submission

pages.name: About
pages.description: Informational page about the game and the official news site.
primary_functions: Explain the purpose of the site and its relationship to the game
Introduce the development and community teams behind the content
Link to key sections such as Support and official community channels

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Support' or 'Help Center' link on the About page
Click the site logo in the header to return to Home

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "About" link in the site footer
May be linked from external community channels as the site introduction

pages.name: Support
pages.description: Help and support page for issues related to the news site and its features.
primary_functions: Provide FAQs on using the site (e.g., managing Reading List, Favorites, Collections, and Notifications)
Offer guidance for troubleshooting common problems (e.g., not receiving emails)
Provide contact options or links for further assistance

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a help link about managing email updates within the Support page
Click a help link about notification settings within the Support page
Click the site logo in the header to return to Home

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Support" link in the site footer
Linked from external support/contact channels

pages.name: Privacy Policy
pages.description: Legal page describing how user data and preferences (like email and notifications) are handled.
primary_functions: Explain data collection and usage practices related to newsletters, notifications, and site usage
Describe user rights regarding their data and privacy choices
Provide contact information for privacy-related inquiries

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Contact support' or similar link from the Privacy Policy

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Privacy Policy" link in the footer
Frequently linked from account or legal communications

pages.name: Terms of Service
pages.description: Legal terms governing use of the official news and updates site.
primary_functions: Present terms and conditions for using the website and its content
Outline rules for user-generated content such as comments
Specify legal limitations of liability and acceptable use policies

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Contact' or 'Report an issue' link from the Terms page

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Terms of Service" link in the footer
Linked from legal notices and user agreements

header_links.description: Homepage with latest news and quick links to all sections
Browse and filter all game patch notes
Esports news, including tournaments and events
Esports listing focused on tournament announcements
Gameplay guides and how-to content for all player levels
View and manage custom article collections (e.g., Roadmap Reading, New Players)
Access bookmarked articles saved for later reading
Manage email newsletter subscription and topic preferences
Configure notification categories, delivery methods, and quiet hours

footer_links.description: Information about the game and the official news site
Help center and contact options for site-related issues
Sign up for or manage email updates
Details on how your data and preferences are handled
Legal terms for using the website
